metadata.name: Global Tariff Briefing
metadata.audience: NYT colleagues tracking tariffs and trade policy globally

all.headline: HORMUZ TRACKER: Iran-Linked Ships Transit as Others Stay Away
all.description: Strait of Hormuz transit remains near a standstill for a sixth day, with Iran-linked tankers the only large vessels making the crossing in the past 24 hours.

all.headline: Fixed Income Investing: Masters in Business with Ed Perks
all.description: Barry speaks with Ed Perks, president of Franklin Advisers, Inc. and chief investment officer of Franklin Income Investors. They discuss income based investment compared to equities, and overall portf

all.headline: Global Economy Faces Widening Strains as Mideast War Intensifies
all.description: The economic fallout from the war in the Mideast is spreading outside the region.

all.headline: Henry Blodget on the Software Selloff Hysteria and the Problem for OpenAI
all.description: How AI will affect both media and software.

all.headline: Odd Lots: Henry Blodget on the Problem for OpenAI (Podcast)
all.description: A year ago, all of the talk was about how the big AI companies were wildly overvalued. Everyone was calling it a bubble. Fast forward to now, and a dominant idea in the markets is that AI is so powerf

all.headline: Energy Price Shock From Iran War Exposes Europe’s Weakness
all.description: Underneath the glass and wrought iron ceiling of Antwerp’s stock exchange last month, French President Emmanuel Macron addressed a hall full of executives from heavy industries. He wasted no time in d

all.headline: In Charts: How The Iran Conflict is Disrupting Global Trade
all.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz and airspace across the Gulf has rippled through the global economy, pushing up oil and fertilizer prices.

all.headline: ECB Rate-Hike Bets Test Officials’ Attempts to Keep Their Cool
all.description: European Central Bank efforts to project calm over war and inflation are being tested as markets price in a hike in interest rates this year.

all.headline: Saudi Arabia Intercepts Drones Heading Toward Shaybah Oil Field
all.description: Saudi Arabia intercepted drones in the Empty Quarter desert that were heading toward the Shaybah oil field, which has the capacity to produce 1 million barrels of crude a day, according to state-run S

all.headline: India Raises LPG Prices as Hormuz Crisis Chokes Flows
all.description: India raised prices of its most widely used cooking gas for the first time in a year, as the Iran war disrupted Middle East energy flows and sent global fuel costs higher.

all.headline: Oil price at two-year high after Qatar warns all Gulf production could stop within days
all.description: Energy Minister Saad al-Kaabi says oil could hit $150 a barrel if the Iran conflict continues over the coming weeks.

all.headline: Flight paths squeezed as Iran conflict closes more airspace
all.description: A drone attack on Azerbaijan has narrowed choices for airlines scrambling to respond to disruption in the Gulf.

all.headline: US economy unexpectedly sheds 92,000 jobs in February
all.description: The contraction came as a surprise with payrolls down in nearly every sector.

all.headline: Axel Springer agrees to buy Telegraph Media Group in £575m deal
all.description: Last year the Daily Mail and General Trust proposed to buy the company in a £500m takeover.

all.headline: Surge in jet fuel prices could push up air fares, analysts warn
all.description: Disruption to supplies from the Gulf due to the Middle East conflict has pushed the cost up by more than 80%.

all.headline: Five ways the Iran war could affect you - in charts
all.description: With fuel and gas prices having risen in recent days, here are some ways the conflict could affect households.

all.headline: Price of first class stamps to rise to £1.80
all.description: Royal Mail says the increase reflects the rise in delivery costs, but the move angers consumer groups.

all.headline: Lenders lift mortgage rates as Iran war hits borrowing costs
all.description: Nationwide, HSBC and Coventry Building Society are all putting some mortgage rates up.

all.headline: Will petrol and diesel prices go up now?
all.description: If oil prices remain high for some time, there could be knock-on effects on the cost of fuel and food.

all.headline: Emirates resumes some Dubai flights – what's the latest on travel to UK?
all.description: New flights to the UK from the Middle East follow days of widespread air travel disruption which had left Britons stranded.

all.headline: Trump demands 'unconditional surrender' from Iran as Putin speaks with Iran's president
all.description: The president also calls on Tehran to pick new leaders who are "acceptable" to the White House after six days of war.

all.headline: Armed robots take to the battlefield in Ukraine war
all.description: Ukraine has embarked on a programme to deploy armed robots on the battlefield against Russian forces.

all.headline: We've been speaking to Iranians during one week of war. Here's what they said
all.description: Ordinary Iranians reflect on seven days of conflict and where they see their country going next.

all.headline: Israeli troops push into Lebanon for yet another war with Iran's proxy Hezbollah
all.description: The BBC sees troops and tanks massing near the border, as speculation mounts that Israel is planning a full-scale ground invasion to fight Hezbollah.

all.headline: 'Massive' numbers killed by gunmen in latest Nigeria attack, senator tells BBC
all.description: Suspected Islamist militants reportedly seized control of a village for two days before being forced out by troops and air strikes.

all.headline: Russian strike on Kharkiv apartment block kills seven
all.description: Ukraine President Volodymyr Zelensky says ten other people - including children - are injured as rescuers continue to search through rubble.

all.headline: China spent years building ties in Latin America. Can Trump make room for the US?
all.description: US President Donald Trump will meet with ideologically-aligned Latin American leaders to try and counter China's influence in the region.

all.headline: Swiss to vote on right-wing push to slash licence fee for public broadcaster
all.description: The move is backed by the right-wing Swiss People's Party, which says the current fee is unjustified because of the high cost of living.

all.headline: Indonesia to ban social media and other online platforms for under 16s
all.description: It is the latest country to curb young people's access to digital spaces to try and protect them from abuse.

all.headline: Dutch prime minister says strikes on Iran not in line with international law
all.description: “Both the attacks on Iran last week and the unacceptable retaliation from Iran on countries in the region fall outside the framework of international law,” Rob Jetten said.

all.headline: US Justice Department publishes documents with sexual assault allegations against Trump
Germany’s pro-business liberals risk death blow in regional vote
all.description: The Free Democratic Party has played a key role in German politics for most of the postwar era, but a poor result in a state vote on Sunday could mean its demise.

all.headline: Hungary’s election campaign is about to get even dirtier
all.description: Now that the candidates are locked in, analysts expect smear campaigns and influence operations to intensify.

all.headline: Jet fuel prices hit 28-month high as Iran war squeezes EU airlines
all.description: European carriers are also wary that a crucial air passage over Azerbaijan could be closed.

all.headline: Ukraine offers drone know-how for Gulf cash and US air defenses
all.description: Kyiv hopes that the knowledge it has gained fighting Russia will give it both money and leverage with Donald Trump.

all.headline: All March EU meetings in Cyprus rescheduled or made virtual
all.description: “Due to developments in the region there were difficulties or cancellations with the flights not only to Cyprus, but also to other countries in the region, so we had to be flexible,” a Cypriot officia

all.headline: Von der Leyen doubles down in face of energy crisis criticism
all.description: EU member countries have been spooked by stubbornly high prices and a new war in the Middle East.

all.headline: Battle between Polish president and prime minister deepens over EU defense loans
all.description: Warsaw's deep political divisions risk torpedoing the country's access to €44 billion in cheap EU rearmament loans.

primary.headline: HORMUZ TRACKER: Iran-Linked Ships Transit as Others Stay Away
primary.description: Strait of Hormuz transit remains near a standstill for a sixth day, with Iran-linked tankers the only large vessels making the crossing in the past 24 hours.

primary.headline: In Charts: How The Iran Conflict is Disrupting Global Trade
primary.description: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz and airspace across the Gulf has rippled through the global economy, pushing up oil and fertilizer prices.

primary.headline: ECB Rate-Hike Bets Test Officials’ Attempts to Keep Their Cool
primary.description: European Central Bank efforts to project calm over war and inflation are being tested as markets price in a hike in interest rates this year.

primary.headline: India Raises LPG Prices as Hormuz Crisis Chokes Flows
primary.description: India raised prices of its most widely used cooking gas for the first time in a year, as the Iran war disrupted Middle East energy flows and sent global fuel costs higher.

secondary.headline: Oil price at two-year high after Qatar warns all Gulf production could stop within days
